verdict_reason: The disagreement entry on the four-element denial structure (kledayanti / śoṣayati) names the doctrinal stake precisely. Translations preserve the four-element structure but flatten the structural argument. Shankara reads the denials structurally — each denial is keyed to a different mode of decomposition, and the Self lacks the property each mode requires. Ramanuja reads them topologically — the Self pervades the elements rather than being pervadable. The explanation captures three distinct facts: (a) translations preserve form but flatten argument; (b) the four denials are structurally distinct, not interchangeable rhetorical flourishes; (c) the two readings are compatible-but-different (structural property vs. topological pervasion). Score 9, PASS.
